title: Altensteinia weberbaueri
intro: Altensteinia weberbaueri is a perennial herb belonging to the Gentianaceae family, native to the Andean region. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing, reducing fever, and treating various infections. Bioactive constituents of Altensteinia weberbaueri include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas and infusions, as well as being incorporated into tinctures and ointments, which are used to treat various ailments, including skin conditions and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Altensteinia weberbaueri is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. It is native to Peru and is known to be endemic to certain regions. This plant is classified within the genus Altensteinia and is further classified under the family Gentianaceae. The family Gentianaceae is a large family of flowering plants commonly known as the gentian or gentianwort family. Altensteinia weberbaueri is a part of this family and is closely related to other plants within the Gentianaceae family.
